title: Intensely Energizing Tandoor Naan
description: A traditional Chinese-inspired dish featuring a dark brown sugar and energizing ingredients, cooked in a clay pot tandoor to perfection. This recipe is anti-inflammatory-friendly and perfect for a snack or dessert.

ingredients.name: Tandoor wheat flour
ingredients.notes: Make sure to use a high-protein type for best results.

ingredients.name: Activated charcoal powder
ingredients.notes: To give the naan its distinctive dark color.

ingredients.name: Psyllium husk
ingredients.notes: For a boost of fiber and nutrition.

ingredients.name: Coconut oil
ingredients.notes: For a creamy and dairy-free dough.

ingredients.name: Paprika
ingredients.notes: To add a smoky depth to the naan.

ingredients.name: Turmeric
ingredients.notes: For its anti-inflammatory properties and bright yellow color.

instructions: Combine the wheat flour, Epsom salt, activated charcoal powder, psyllium husk, and salt in a large mixing bowl.
Add the dark brown sugar, coconut oil, and water to the bowl and mix until a shaggy dough forms.
Knead the dough for 10 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Place the dough in a greased tandoor or clay pot and let it ferment for 1 hour.
Preheat the tandoor or clay pot to 450°F (230°C).
Shape the dough into small balls and flatten them slightly into disks.
Place the naan disks in the preheated tandoor or clay pot and cook for 2-3 minutes on each side.
Brush the naan with coconut oil and sprinkle with sesame seeds and fresh cilantro.
Serve the naan warm and enjoy!
Repeat the process to make more naan disks.
Make sure to not overcrowd the tandoor or clay pot.
